gates.step0_smoke: PASS - all 4 corruption modes run without crash, finite loss, on real IN-1K
gates.step2_mask_sanity: PENDING (eval metrics) - loss-curve comparison RETRACTED as gate evidence: raw train losses are NOT comparable across corruption modes. Linear-path target ut = x1 - x0; with mask x0 = m*x1+(1-m)*eps, so x1-x0 = (1-m)*(x1-eps) is zero on kept pixels; at mask_prob=0.5 expected MSE ~= half of gaussian arm's, which exactly matches observed (~5.2 vs ~11.2). The 2x gap measures task easiness, not landscape quality. Full-epoch completion (both arms, 40000 steps, no crash) does confirm training stability. Gate now requires outcome metrics on the v3 checkpoints: FID from pure-noise sampling, energy ordering E(clean)<E(corrupt)<E(noise), and/or denoising recovery vs baseline.
